title: Proclamation No. 461, s. 1939
text:
MALACAÑAN PALACE MANILA

PROCLAMATION NO. 461

RESERVING FOR SCHOOL PURPOSES FIVE PARCELS OF THE PUBLIC DOMAIN SITUATED IN THE BARRIOS OF POBLACION, LUBON, MASLA, SUMADEL, MUNICIPAL DISTRICT OF KAYAN, AND IN THE BARRIO OF PANABUÑGEN, MUNICIPAL DISTRICT OF BESAO, SUBPROVINCE OF BONTOC, MOUNTAIN PROVINCE, ISLAND OF LUZON.

Upon the recommendation of the Secretary of Agriculture and Commerce and pursuant to the provisions of section eighty-three of Commonwealth Act Numbered One hundred and forty-one, as amended, I hereby withdraw from sale or settlement and reserve for school purposes, under the administration of the Director of Education, subject to private rights, if any there be, the following parcels of the public domain, situated in the barrios of Poblacion, Lubon, Masla, Sumadel, municipal district of Kayan, and in the barrio of Panabuñgen, municipal district of Besao, sub-province of Bontoc, Mountain Province, Island of Luzon, and particularly described in Bureau of Lands plans Psu-103366, sheets Nos. 1, 2, 3, and 4 and Psu-103367, to wit:

A parcel of land (plan Psu-103367, G. L. R. 0. Record No.         ), situated in the barrio of Panabuñgen, municipal district of Besao, Mountain Province (subprovince of Bontoc), Island of Luzon. Bounded on the N. by the property of the heirs of Batiano and public land; on the NE., by the properties of the heirs of Batiano, Labayan, and Banglati; on the S., by the properties of Manang and Tuito; and on the W., by the properties of Modesto et al., Rosalino Dogaong, and public land; beginning at a point marked “1” on plan, being N. 14° 08′ W . 10,224.19 meters, more or less, from B. L. L. M. No. 1, municipal district of Kayan, thence N. 5° 39′ W. 60.97 m. to point “2;” thence , N. 4° 53′ E. 24.67 m. to point “3;” thence N. 0° 02′ E.  27.00. m. to point “4;” thence N. 20° 03′ E. 48.40 m. to point “5;” thence N. 11° 35′ E. 40.60 m. to point “6;” thence “S. 78° 27′ E. 75.38 m. to point “7;” thence S. 58° 46′ E. 60.14 m. to point “8;” thence S. 29° 01′ E. 28.19 m. to point “9;” thence S. 13° 52′ E. 37.74 m. to point “10;” thence S. 23° 20′ E. 72.71 m. to point “11;” thence S. 85° 14′ W. 124.90 m. to point “12;” thence S. .80° 05′ W. 74.28 m. to the point of beginning; containing an area of twenty-eight thousand and sixty-five (28,065) square meters, more or less. All points referred to are indicated on the plan, and on the ground points “1” to “12,” inclusive, are marked by P. L. S. stone monuments; 15 by 15 by 60 cms.; bearings true; declination, 0° 30′ E.; date of survey, April 4-16, 1937; and that of the approval, October 22, 1937.

Lot No. 1 (Kayan Central School site). —A parcel of land (plan Psu-103366, lot No. 1, G. L. R. O. Record No.          ), situated in the Poblacion, municipal district of Kayan, Mountain Province (subprovince of Bontoc), Island of Luzon. Bounded on the N. by road; on the E., by the properties of Dionisio V. Sacay-ap, Butigan, Tumapang, Tuctucan, and public land; on the SW., by public land; and on the NW., by public land and property of Ignacio Marrero; beginning at a point marked “1” on plan, being N. 88° 05′ W. 745.90 m., more or less, from B. L. L. M. No. 1, municipal district of Kayan, thence S. 20° 07′ W. 41.14 m. to point “2;” thence S. 23° 05′ W. 31.11 m. to point “3;” thence S. 28° 56′ W. 32.98 m. to point “4;” thence S. 22° 46′ W. 52.22 m. to point “5;” thence S. 27° 14′ W. 180.63 m. to point “6;” thence N. 69° 32′ W. 199.67 m. to point “7;” thence N. 41° 29′ W. 98.74 m. to point “8;” thence N. 25° 36′ E. 34.54 m. to poinl_”9;”_ thence N. 69° 51′ E. 139.61 m. to point “10;” thence N. 15° 03′ W. 17.90 m. to point “11;” thence N. 70° 50′ E. 92.19 m. to point “12;” thence N. 8° 06′ W . 42.56 m. to point “13;” thence N. 82° 26′ E. 81.94 m. to point “14;” thence S. 84° 34′ E. 84.66 m. to point “15;” thence-S. 42° 43′ E. 14.39 m. to the point of beginning; containing an area of sixty-nine thousand and ninety-five (69,095) square meters, more or less. All points referred to are indicated on the plan, and on the ground are marked by P. L. S. stone monuments, 30 by 40 by 60 cms.; bearings true, declination, 0° 30′ E.; date of survey, April 4-16, 1937, and that of the approval, October 22, 1937.

Lot No. 2 (Lubon -Barrio School site). —A parcel of land (lot No. 2 of plan Psu-103366, G. L. R. O. Record No.         ), situated in the barrio of-Lubon, municipal district of Kayan, Mountain” Province “(subprovince of Bontoc), Island of Luzon. Bounded on the N. by the properties of Eduardo Astudillo, Enrique Astolfo, and Santiago Valenciano; on the E., by the property of Kapuyan; on the S., by the property of Esteban Perfecto; and on the SW., by the properties of Paklay and Saro (Infiel) ; and on the “W., by properties of Saro (Infiel), Gregorio Cardenas, and Jose Marrero. Beginning at a point marked “1” on plan, being N. 3° 07′ E.. 2,875.73 m., more or less, from B. L. L. M. No. 1, municipal district of Kayan, thence N. 30° 52′ W. 23.24 m. to point “2;” thence N. 27° 40′ W. 15.12 m. to point “3;” thence N. 4° 02′ W. 13.50 m. to point “4;” thence N. 5° 00′ E. 10.79 m. to point “5;” thence N. 0° 53′ W. 24.21 m. to point “6;” thence N. 89° 37′ E. 22.62 m. to point “7;” thence S. 83° 05′ E. 20.00 m. to point “8;” thence S. 83° 04′ E. 26.50 m. to point “9;” thence S. 5° 35′ W. 87.67 m. to point “10;” thence N. 75° 02′ W. 42.37 m. to the point of beginning; containing an area of five thousand one hundred and twenty-eight (5,128) square meters, more or less. All points referred to are indicated on the plan, and on the ground points “1” and “4” to “10,” inclusive, are marked by P. L. S. stone monuments, 15 by 15 by 60 cms.; points “2” and “3,” by a nail on “X” on trees, 45 cms. in diameter; bearings true; declination, 0° 30′ E.; date of survey, April 4-16, 1937, and that of the approval, October 22, 1937.

Lot No. 3 (Masla Barrio School site). —A parcel of land (lot No. 3 of plan Psu-103366, G. L. R. O. Record No.         ), situated in the barrio of Masla, municipal district of Kayan, Mountain Province (subprovince of Bontoc), Island of Luzon. Bounded on the N. by the properties of Letong, Anayen, Tadlangan, Docles, and public land; on the E., by public land; on the SE., by public land and barrio road 6 m. wide; and on the W., by the properties of Tadlangan, Wapaw, Gabaen, Salvador Tamking, and Pandayas. Beginning at a p’oint marked “1” on plan, being N. 15° 46′ E. 4,211.90 m. from B. L. L. M. No. 1, municipal district of Kayan, thence S. 52° 50′ W. 32.23 m. to point “2;” thence S. 49° 08′ W. 43.61 m. to point “3;” thence N. 40° 06′ W. 20.69 m. to point “4;” thence N. 11° 46′ W. 10.64 m. to point “5;” thence N. 19° 09′ E. 18.08 m. to point “6;” thence N. 26° 25′ “W. 15.89 m. to point “7;” thence N. 40° 04′ W. 7.74 m. to point “8;” thence N. 12° 20′ “W. 25.10 m. to point “9; thence N. 6° 34′ W. 11.71 m. to point “10;” thence N. 6° 22′ W. 17.96 m. to point “11;” thence N. 68° 28′ E. 12.40 m. to point “12;” thence N. 70° 58′ E. 6.75 m. to point “13;” thence N. 76° 09′ E. 9.77 m. to point “14;” thence N. 71° 34′ E. 19.15 m. to point “15;” thence N. 68° 25′ E. 26.84 m. to point “16;” thence S. 11° 03′ E. 96.28 m. to the point of beginning; containing an area of eight thousand and ninety-seven (8,097) square meters more or less. All points referred to are indicated on the plan, and on the ground points “1,” “2,” “3,” “5,” “13,” “14,” and “16” are marked by P. L. S. stone monuments, 15 by 15 by 60 cms.; points “4,” “11,” and “15,” by old B. L. stone monuments, 15 by 15 by 60 cms.; points “6,” “7,” “8,” “9,” “10,” and “12,” by P. L. S. stone monuments, 20 by 20 by 60 cms.; bearings true, declination, 0° 30′ E.; date of survey, April 4-16, 1937, and that of the approval, October 22, 1937.

Lot No. 4 (Sumadel Barrio School site) .—A parcel of land (lot No. 4 of plan Psu-103366, G. L. R. O. Record No.       ), situated in the barrio of Sumadel, municipal district of Kayan, Mountain Province (subprovince of Bon-toc, Island of Luzon. Bounded on the N. by lot No. 5; on the E., by lot No. 5; on the SW., by Imbakan Creek, 6-10 m. wide; and on the W., by properties of Macario Piluden et al. and Alitan Angtengan. Beginning at a point marked “1” on plan, being N. 25° 34′ E. 4,830.78, more or less, from B. L. L. M. No. 1, municipal district of Kayan, thence S. 5° 41′ E. 45.46 m. to point “2;” thence N. 38° 44′ W. 77.40 m. to point “3;” thence N. 2° 01′ W. 47.69 m. to point “4;” thence N. 11° 36′ E. 17.91 m. to point “5;” thence N. 87° 02′ E. 58.63 m. to point “6;” thence S. 26° 58′ W. 25.38 m. to point “7;” thence S. 3° 54′ E. 13.82 m. to point “8;” thence S. 3° 03′ E. 32.62 m. to point “9;” thence S. 28° 11′ “W. 16.33 m. to point of beginning; containing an area of four thousand eight hundred thirty-one (4,831) square meters, more or less. All points referred to are indicated on the plan, and on the ground points “1” and “7” are marked by old B. L. stone monuments, 20 by 30 by 60 cms.; points “2,” “5,” “6,” “8,” and “9,” by P. L. S. stone monuments, 15 by 15 by 60 cms.; points “3” and “4,” by an old stone boulder; bearings true; declination, 0° 30′ E.; date of survey, April 4-16, 1937, and that of the approval, October 22, 1937.

Lot No. 5 (Sumadel Barrio School site). —A parcel of land (lot No. 5 of plan Psu-103366, G. L. R. O. Record No.        ), situated in the barrio of Sumadel, municipal district of Kayan, Mountain Province (subprovince of Bontoc), Island of Luzon. Bounded on the N. by the property of Macario et al.; on the E., by the properties of Arsenio Jbs.e et al. and Libay et al.; on the SE., by the property of Arsenio Jose et al.; and on the S. and W., by lot No. 4; and on the NW., by property of Macario et al. Beginning at a point marked “1” on plan, being N. 25° 34′ E., 4,830.78 m., more or less, from B. L. L. M. No. 1, municipal district of Kayan, thence N. 28° 11′ E, 16.33 m. to point “2;” thence N. 3° 03′ W. 32.62 m. to point “3;” thence N. 3° 54′ W. 13.82 m. to point “4;” thence N. 26′ 58′ E. 25.38 m. to point “5;” thence S. 87° 02′ W. 58.63 m. to point “6;” thence N. 44° 57′ E. 17.92 m. to point “7;” thence S. 82° 12′ E. 46.67 m. to point “8;” thence S. 20° 01′ E. 42.86 m. to point “9;” thence S. 13° 26′ E. 43.31 m. to point “10;” thence S. 36° 56′ W. 62.00 m. to point “11;” thence S. 5° 41′ W. 45.46 m. to the point of beginning; containing an area of three thousand three hundred and thirty-nine (3,339) square meters, more or less. All points referred to are indicated on the plan, and on the ground points “1” and “4” are marked by old B. L. stone monuments, 20 by 30 by 60 cms.; points “2,” “3,” “5,” “6,” “8,” “10,” and “11, by P. L. S. stone monuments, 15 by 15 by 60 cms.; points “7” and “9,” by old stone boulder; bearings true; declination, 0° 30′ E.; date of survey, April 4-16, 1937, and that of the approval, October 22, 1937.

In witness whereof, I have hereunto set my hand and caused the seal of the Commonwealth of the Philippines to be affixed.

Done at the City of Manila, this thirtieth day of September, in the year of Our Lord, nineteen hundred and thirty-nine, and of the Commonwealth of the Philippines, the fourth.

MANUEL L. QUEZON President of the Philippines

By the President: JORGE B. VARGAS Secretary to the President

Source : Legislative Library, House of Representatives
